channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Digitalizar y cooperar para… saber y poder cuidar o curar
item.description: La transformación digital que se está produciendo en el sector salud no sigue un esquema planificado, ni responde a una estrategia explícita planteada por alguno de los múltiples actores que interactúan en nuestro sistema sanitario.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cuatro recomendaciones para que los CIO salven la brecha de confianza en la IA
item.description: Por tercer año consecutivo, el Thinkers360 AI Trust Index ha tomado el pulso a la opinión pública sobre la IA y, una vez más, sus resultados recuerdan a los CIO y CXO que la curva de innovación tecnológica sigue avanzando más rápido que la estructura ética y de gobernanza necesaria para sostenerla.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Reader picks: The most popular Python stories of 2025
item.description: Python 3.14 was the star of the show in 2025, bringing official support for free-threaded builds, a new all-in-one installation manager for Windows, and subtler perks like the new template strings feature.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why Apple and Google want your ID
item.description: Apple and Google would like to see your identification, please. With the former’s “Digital ID” launch last week, both companies now let you scan a digital version of your passport at more than 250 Transportation Security Administration checkpoints, using an iPhone or Android phone.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla’s Model 3 emergency door release controls are being investigated
item.description: The U.S. auto safety regulator said on Wednesday it has opened a defect investigation into Tesla Model 3 compact sedans over concerns that emergency door release controls may not be easily accessible or clearly identifiable in an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AI-created ransomware and NFC attacks lead the surge in new cyberattacks - here's how you can stay safe this holidays
item.description: AI is no longer being used just to craft convincing phishing emails - it's being used to build ransomware.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: New malware can read your chats and steal your money
item.description: New Android banking trojan Sturnus steals credentials, reads encrypted messages and controls devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cameroon vs Gabon Free Streams: How to watch AFCON 2025 Group F opener, TV channels, preview
item.description: We'll show you all the ways you can watch Cameroon vs Gabon in the 2025 AFCON from anywhere in the world.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Shapland, TechRadar

item.title: Intel shares fall on report Nvidia halts production test
item.description: Intel Corp. shares fell Wednesday after a report said that Nvidia Corp. halted a test to use Intel's production process to make advanced chips.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump’s $100,000 H-1B visa application fee upheld by judge
item.description: A federal judge said the Trump administration can move ahead with a $100,000 fee on new H-1B visa applications, providing a setback for US technology companies that rely on hiring skilled foreign workers.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: These malicious Google Chrome extensions have stolen data from over 170 sites - find out if you're affected
item.description: Two Chrome extensions were found eavesdropping on people's browsing, stealing login credentials and payment card information.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft is not rewriting Windows in Rust
item.description: A job posting by a Microsoft engineer sparked excitement about a project “to eliminate every line of C and C++ from Microsoft by 2030”, replacing it with Rust — but alas for fans of the memory-safe programming language, it turns out this is a personal goal, not a corporate one, and Rust isn’t necess
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft is not rewriting Windows in Rust
item.description: A job posting by a Microsoft engineer sparked excitement about a project “to eliminate every line of C and C++ from Microsoft by 2030”, replacing it with Rust — but alas for fans of the memory-safe programming language, it turns out this is a personal goal, not a corporate one, and Rust isn’t necess
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: EU warns of possible action after the US bars 5 Europeans accused of censorship
item.description: France, Germany, the European Union and the United Kingdom are hitting out at a U.S. decision to impose travel bans on five Europeans the Trump administration accuses of pressuring tech firms to censor or suppress American views.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 3D-printed housing project for student apartments takes shape
item.description: Denmark's 3D-printed student village proves automation builds 36 apartments faster than traditional methods. Skovsporet project shows housing future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Russian hackers claim cyberattack on French postal service
item.description: French prosecutors say a pro-Russian hacking group has claimed responsibility for a major cyberattack on France’s national postal service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The 5 big events that shaped Microsoft’s 2025
item.description: This has been a pivotal 12 months for Microsoft, a year in which it faced increasing competition that threatened to knock the company off its perch as the world’s leader in AI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FCC bans new foreign-made drones over national security concerns
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission on Monday said it would ban new foreign-made drones, a move that will keep new Chinese-made drones such as those from DJI and Autel out of the U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This AI startup is extending an olive branch between humans and machines
item.description: In the fall of 2024, six college students joined forces to start an AI company together. Five of them had met while studying computer science, computer engineering, and electrical engineering at Georgia Tech in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Android Sound Notifications help you catch key alerts
item.description: Discover Android's new Sound Notifications feature that alerts you to smoke alarms, doorbells, and baby cries even when wearing headphones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Data 2026
item.description: How can agencies manage the challenge of turning data into mission-critical insights — quickly, securely and at scale? The post Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Data 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Expert Edition: How to build cyber resilience for the quantum era
item.description: Discover how federal leaders are tackling zero trust, post-quantum cryptography, identity modernization and AI readiness. Download our Day 2 Cyber Leaders Exchange e-book now! The post Expert Edition: How to build cyber resilience for the quantum era first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: US cyber progress isn’t stalled
item.description: Beneath the policy layer, the technical and strategic modernization of U.S. cybersecurity is actually accelerating faster than ever. The post US cyber progress isn’t stalled — it’s evolving first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Sean Davis, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple appears set to begin iPhone 18 (test) production soon
item.description: Something that appears to have started earlier than ever before, Apple is allegedly already test manufacturing of the next model of its basic smartphone, the iPhone 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Data center investments break all records
item.description: New data centers are being built around the world in connection with AI initiatives. According to a compilation by S&P Global, spending will reach a record $61 billion in 2025, with the US and Canada accounting for the vast majority.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Global uncertainty is reshaping cloud strategies in Europe
item.description: When Austria’s Ministry of Economy, Energy, and Tourism set out to replace Skype for Business as its virtual meeting platform, Microsoft Teams was the obvious choice.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FBI warns of fake kidnapping photos used in new scam
item.description: FBI warns cybercriminals are stealing family photos from social media to create fake proof of life images in virtual kidnapping scams targeting victims.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
